steps.title: Identify the parlor's dual demands
steps.content: The front parlor is Tia Ana's classroom space, but every evening it becomes Salomé's salon and—critically—is never in order for its transformation back to a classroom the following morning. This reveals she has preparation work waiting.

steps.title: Match her behavior to the answer
steps.content:
Her repeated checks whether guests have left show she needs the space back to prepare it.

Choice C captures this exactly: she's anxious for the gathering to disperse so she can ready the space for her own needs.

The other answers either invent unsupported motives (A and B) or point to a separate household issue (D).

That matches choice C — C is correct.
